explanation: PowerShell allows parameter truncation. By supplying '-bin' instead of the full '-BinaryPathName', the command functions identically but evades the exact string match required by the Sigma rule.

PowerShell treats the backtick (`) as an escape character and ignores it if it doesn't precede a recognized escape sequence. Inserting a backtick into 'New-Service' breaks the string signature while allowing the cmdlet to execute.
By concatenating the cmdlet string and invoking it via the call operator (&), the exact string 'New-Service' never appears in its complete form in the command line arguments.
